findings.title: Missing proposal.md error uses wrong error code
findings.detail: When proposal.md is not found (line 383), the error code is `missing_baseline` with capability `<proposal.md>`. This conflates a missing proposal with a missing baseline spec. Consider using a distinct error code (e.g. `missing_proposal`) or at minimum documenting that this is an intentional reuse. The spec only defines `missing_baseline` and `unparseable_baseline` as error codes, so this is technically out-of-spec but harmless since a missing proposal.md would be caught earlier in the workflow.

findings.title: findRippleCandidates uses string.includes for title matching
findings.detail: Line 349 uses `lines[i].includes(title)` which can produce false positives if a requirement title is a common substring (e.g. title 'State' would match any line containing that word). This is acceptable since the spec says the agent judges whether each candidate is actually broken, but a word-boundary check would reduce noise. Low priority since the agent filters results.

findings.title: spec_verify phase contract has gated: false
findings.detail: The spec_verify phase contract sets `gated: false` while the proposal describes it as a blocking gate. The semantic gating is enforced by the agent workflow (command body), not the phase contract field, so this is consistent with how spec_validate works. No action needed unless the project's convention for `gated` means something different.
